agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment and premium fee. Sourced from a multi-stakeholder product discovery workshop with unresolved contradictions between Product, Operations, Finance, Legal, and Customer Success.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a time-window commitment (target: 2 hours) and an additional fee, available to eligible customers within range of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The premium surcharge ($12.99) charged on top of the base delivery fee for express service." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee charged for all deliveries, separate from any express surcharge." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The time-bound commitment (currently 2 hours from order placement) within which an express delivery is targeted for completion." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The internal operational target requiring an express order to be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of placement." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Radius", "definition": "The maximum distance (10km) between customer delivery address and the nearest warehouse for express eligibility." },
      { "term": "Driver Rating Threshold", "definition": "The minimum driver rating (4 stars) required to be assigned express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Missed Window Refund", "definition": "A goodwill credit of the express fee issued when the delivery window is not met, subject to exclusion conditions (e.g., wrong customer address)." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation", "definition": "When an assigned express driver cancels mid-delivery, triggering a replacement assignment protocol." },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The maximum time (10 minutes) allowed to assign a replacement driver after a cancellation before an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Real-Time Tracking", "definition": "Live location visibility of the assigned express driver, available to the customer throughout the delivery." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — beyond 10km the delivery window is operationally impossible. Allowing orders outside this radius would create systemic SLA failures.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address more than 10km from any warehouse, the express delivery option must not be selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by product/finance leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer selects express delivery, the order total must include both the base delivery fee and a $12.99 express surcharge as separate line items."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational SLA target that may be tuned based on market conditions, though violating it degrades the delivery window commitment.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order placed at time T, the system must dispatch and have a driver pick up the order by T+15 minutes, or escalate."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or higher may be assigned express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality threshold that operations leadership could adjust. Not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with a rating below 4.0 stars, the driver must not appear in the express-eligible driver pool."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the delivery window is missed, the express fee is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund policy is a business decision, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express delivery completed after the 2-hour window, a refund equal to exactly the express fee ($12.99) must be issued; the base delivery fee must remain charged."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No missed-window refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund exclusion policy that could be revised by customer success or legal.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed delivery window where the root cause is flagged as 'incorrect customer address', no express fee refund must be issued."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery and no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Compensation policy, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation at time T on an express delivery with no replacement driver assigned by T+10 minutes, the system must automatically initiate an express fee refund without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "The express delivery time commitment is presented as a target/best-effort, not a contractual guarantee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Legal compliance constraint — presenting it as a guarantee could create contractual liability. Violating this exposes the company to legal risk.",
      "testable_assertion": "All customer-facing copy referencing the 2-hour delivery window must include qualifying language (e.g., 'target', 'best effort') and must not use the word 'guarantee' or 'guaranteed'."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is the 10km boundary inclusive or exclusive? Must be defined. Recommend inclusive (<=10km)." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4 stars mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "Current delivery should be completed by the assigned driver. Rating threshold applies at assignment time, not during delivery." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers available within pickup SLA", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the order be rejected at placement, queued, or downgraded to standard delivery with a refund of the express fee?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after placing express order", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — if new address is >10km from warehouse, does the order lose express status? Who bears the window risk?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple driver cancellations on the same order", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the 10-minute replacement window restart with each cancellation, or is it cumulative? At what point does the system give up?" },
    { "scenario": "Missed window AND wrong address flagged simultaneously", "expected_behavior": "No refund — the wrong-address exclusion (BR-6) takes precedence per Finance's stated policy." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 115 of a 120-minute window", "expected_behavior": "Replacement window (10 min) would exceed the delivery window. The auto-refund at T+10 minutes of cancellation should still apply per BR-7, independent of the missed-window refund." },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse goes offline or runs out of inventory after express order placement", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — no stakeholder addressed supply-side failures. Refund policy and customer communication are undefined." },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 11:50 PM — does the 2-hour window span midnight or operating hours?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — operating hours for express delivery are not defined. Late-night and holiday availability is undefined." },
    { "scenario": "Who determines 'wrong address' and when?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — the process for flagging an address as incorrect is undefined. Driver-reported? System-detected? Appeals process?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) is a direct revenue uplift per eligible order. Missed-window refunds and auto-refunds on driver cancellations are direct cost. The refund exclusion for wrong addresses protects against abuse. Unclear refund automation vs. manual processing creates operational cost risk.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the eligible driver pool. Driver cancellation replacement within 10 minutes requires real-time dispatch optimization. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure and bandwidth requirements.",
    "user": "Prominent display of 2-hour target improves conversion for time-sensitive customers. Real-time tracking increases trust. However, Legal's constraint against calling it a 'guarantee' may confuse customers if Product and Marketing use that language.",
    "risk": "PRIMARY RISK: The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the highest-priority item. If customer-facing materials say 'guaranteed' while legal terms say 'best effort', the company faces both legal liability and customer trust erosion. SECONDARY RISK: Tight operational constraints (15 min pickup, 10 min replacement, 4-star drivers) may make the product undeliverable in markets with thin driver supply.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Premium service expectation set by 'guarantee' language; confusion if refund is discretionary rather than automatic; real-time tracking is a value-add." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "4-star threshold excludes lower-rated drivers from express earnings; express assignments carry tighter time pressure and cancellation visibility." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "Must build systems for 15-minute pickup enforcement, 10-minute replacement assignment, and real-time driver tracking. Escalation paths for missed SLAs are undefined." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Must support automated refunds (per Customer Success) vs. 3-business-day processing (per Finance's own statement). Reconciliation of express fees and refunds needs clear accounting." },
      { "group": "Legal / Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing copy to ensure no contractual guarantee language. Refund framed as goodwill, not obligation." },
      { "group": "Customer Success", "impact": "Needs clear playbook for edge cases: wrong address disputes, multiple cancellations, borderline-distance orders." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a direct contradiction between Product ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / not a guarantee') that fundamentally changes the nature of the product and all downstream rules. Additionally, Finance describes a manual 3-business-day refund process while Customer Success requires automatic refunds — these are incompatible without resolution. Multiple critical edge cases (driver availability, operating hours, wrong-address adjudication, address changes) are entirely unaddressed. This is a raw discovery output, not a resolved spec.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) — all stakeholders agree",
      "10km warehouse radius eligibility — stated by PM, no contradictions",
      "4-star driver rating threshold — stated by Operations, no contradictions",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded — PM and Finance align",
      "Wrong address excludes refund — Finance stated, no contradictions"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Guarantee vs. best-effort framing — direct contradiction between PM and Legal",
      "Refund processing: automatic vs. 3-business-day manual — contradiction between Customer Success and Finance",
      "What happens when no eligible drivers are available",
      "Operating hours and availability windows for express",
      "Wrong address determination process and appeals",
      "Whether the 2-hour window starts at order placement or pickup"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour delivery window starts at order placement, not at pickup (PM said 'within 2 hours' without specifying start point)",
      "The 10km radius is calculated as straight-line distance, not road distance (unspecified)",
      "Driver rating is a cumulative/rolling average, not per-delivery (unspecified)",
      "Warehouse assignment is based on nearest warehouse to customer, not merchant-specific (unspecified)"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Product Manager describes the 2-hour delivery window as 'guaranteed', while Legal explicitly states it cannot be a guarantee in writing — it must be a 'best effort' SLA. This contradiction affects all customer-facing language, the refund policy framing (goodwill vs. obligation), and potentially the product name itself ('Express Delivery' vs. 'Guaranteed Express').",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal / Compliance", "Product", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Adopt Legal's position — frame as 'target' delivery time with goodwill refund. Requires Product and Marketing to revise all language.",
        "B: Negotiate with Legal for limited guarantee language with sufficient disclaimers and caps on liability.",
        "C: Create two tiers of language — internal SLA is 'guarantee' for operational purposes, external language is 'target' or 'aim'."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A. Legal risk from written guarantees is asymmetric — the downside of a class-action or regulatory action far outweighs the conversion benefit of 'guarantee' language. Use 'Express Delivery — 2-hour target' with a goodwill refund policy."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refunds on driver cancellation (no replacement in 10 min) should be 'automatic', while Finance says all missed-window refunds are 'processed within 3 business days'. These are fundamentally different system behaviors — one requires real-time automated refund initiation, the other implies manual batch processing.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Success", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: All express refunds are automatic and instant (credit to account/wallet), with financial reconciliation happening in batch within 3 business days.",
        "B: All express refunds are manually reviewed and processed within 3 business days.",
        "C: Driver-cancellation refunds are automatic (time-critical, customer-visible); missed-window refunds are processed within 3 business days (can be batch-reviewed)."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Driver cancellation is a service failure visible to the customer in real-time — delaying the refund 3 days erodes trust. Missed-window refunds can tolerate batch processing since the delivery is already complete. This also gives Finance a review window to apply the wrong-address exclusion."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 2-hour delivery window start time is undefined. PM says 'within 2 hours' — but from order placement? From pickup? From dispatch confirmation? Operations' 15-minute pickup SLA implies the window includes pickup time, but this is not explicit. If the window starts at placement, 15 minutes of the 2 hours is consumed by pickup alone.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Window starts at order placement (customer's mental model — they placed the order, clock starts).",
        "B: Window starts at pickup confirmation (operational model — the delivery phase is what's being promised)."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A. Customers will measure from when they placed the order. Any other start point creates a perception gap that will drive complaints."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when no express-eligible drivers (4+ stars, within range) are available at order placement or within the 15-minute pickup window. This is a critical operational scenario — rejecting the order loses revenue, accepting it risks an immediate SLA breach.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Product"],
      "options": [
        "A: Do not offer express option when no eligible drivers are available (requires real-time driver availability check at checkout).",
        "B: Accept the order and attempt to find a driver, with a fallback to standard delivery + express fee refund if pickup SLA is missed.",
        "C: Accept the order with a warning ('express drivers are in high demand, we'll do our best')."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A for integrity, with Option B as the fallback if a driver becomes unavailable after order placement. Never charge for a service you cannot deliver."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Operating hours for express delivery are undefined. Is express available 24/7? Only during business hours? Only during warehouse operating hours? An order placed at 11 PM with a 2-hour window implies delivery at 1 AM — is that supported?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express is available only during defined operating hours (e.g., 7 AM–10 PM).",
        "B: Express is available whenever the warehouse is open and drivers are available.",
        "C: Express is 24/7 with surge pricing outside core hours."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Escalate to Operations to define. Cannot set acceptance criteria without this."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The process for determining 'wrong address' (which excludes refund eligibility per BR-6) is entirely undefined. Who makes this determination? The driver? An automated system? Customer Success? Is there an appeals process? Without a clear adjudication process, this exclusion is unenforceable or will be applied inconsistently.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Success", "Legal"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address in-app with photo evidence; Customer Success reviews before applying exclusion.",
        "B: System detects address anomalies (non-existent address, unreachable location) automatically.",
        "C: Both — automated detection plus driver-reported flags, with Customer Success as final arbiter."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Automated detection catches obvious cases, driver reports catch edge cases, and human review prevents abuse of the exclusion."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "The product cannot ship with 'guaranteed' language in customer-facing materials while Legal has explicitly prohibited contractual guarantees.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-8 (Legal compliance — best-effort framing)" },
    { "violation": "The refund mechanism cannot be both 'automatic' and '3 business days' — engineering needs a single source of truth before implementation.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-5 and BR-7 (refund processing)" }
  ]
}
